# migration_name: # phase must be expand, contract, or metadata-only.
# previous_expand_release is required for destructive contract migrations; use n/a otherwise.
# old_code_compatible must be yes for any migration with breaking SQL matched by the deploy guard.

lock_impact_plan: Adds PaymentTransaction plus indexes/FK references to Payment; run during the normal deploy window, verify no long payment transactions before migrate, and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure.

Drops and recreates FK constraints on Booking, Payment, MemberCredit, RefundRequest, and related finance tables without removing columns; run during low traffic, check for long write transactions first, and roll back before traffic cutover if migrate fails.

Adds nullable/default PaymentRefund ledger columns, reads Payment and PaymentTransaction for a bounded backfill, then creates indexes; run during low traffic and verify payment writes are quiet before migrate.

Backfills NominationToken.tokenHash from existing plaintext tokens, recreates unique lookup coverage on tokenHash, then drops the plaintext token column; run during low traffic and verify no active nomination confirmations are in flight before migrate.

Adds nullable Member columns (profileCompletedAt, detailsConfirmedAt, detailsConfirmedByMemberId, onboardingConfirmedAt), a self-referential nullable FK with ON DELETE SET NULL, and a supporting index. Existing rows are not backfilled; old code ignores the new columns and new code sets them on confirmation. ADD COLUMN with no default takes a brief AccessExclusive lock on Member; run during low traffic.

Adds six nullable FamilyGroupJoinRequest columns (requestedFirstName/LastName/DateOfBirth/Email, subjectMemberId, requestNotes), two indexes, and a nullable FK to Member with ON DELETE SET NULL. Old code ignores the new columns; FamilyGroupJoinRequest is low-write so lock impact is minimal.

Backfills legacy Booking rows from CONFIRMED to PAYMENT_PENDING only when no SUCCEEDED Payment exists. This is run after the PAYMENT_PENDING enum migration and after production app containers include a Prisma client that can read the enum. The current affected set is one row; run during low traffic and verify the count before and after migrate.

Backfills legacy Booking rows from CONFIRMED to PAID when a SUCCEEDED Payment exists. Old code already understands PAID bookings, and new code depends on the repaired status for capacity and lodge surfaces. Run during low traffic and verify CONFIRMED bookings grouped by payment status before and after migrate.

Adds nullable IssueReport triage and sensitive-data retention columns plus indexes, then backfills 30-day screenshot/browser-info expiry timestamps for existing rows. Old code ignores the new columns; run during low traffic because IssueReport is low-write and the backfill is bounded by recent support reports.
Creates email message setting, template override, and notification delivery policy tables plus indexes. Old code ignores the new tables; locks are limited to new objects.

Creates payment recovery enums/table/indexes/FKs and backfills risky zero-dollar pending PaymentIntent rows from Booking, Payment, and PaymentTransaction. Old code ignores the queue; run during low traffic and verify pending payment transaction counts before migrate.

Adds BookingGuest stay range columns, backfills from Booking, installs a trigger so old app inserts derive stayStart/stayEnd from the parent booking, then enforces NOT NULL and creates range indexes. BookingGuest is write-active, so run during low traffic, verify no NULL ranges after migrate, and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure.

Adds nullable Member cancellation columns plus membership cancellation request/settings tables, indexes, and FKs. Old code ignores the new columns and tables; run during low traffic because Member and cancellation workflows are low-write.

Adds participant confirmation enum values plus nullable hashed-token and timestamp columns to MembershipCancellationRequestParticipant. Old code ignores the new columns; run during low traffic because membership cancellation requests are low-write, and no existing rows are backfilled.

Adds MemberLifecycleAction and MemberLifecycleActionRequestStatus enums plus the MemberLifecycleActionRequest table for second-admin delete approvals. Old code ignores the new table; run during low traffic because member lifecycle requests are low-write, and no existing rows are backfilled.
Creates BookingChangeRequest enum/table/indexes/FKs for member edit review. Old code ignores the new queue; run during low traffic and rely on the status/createdAt indexes for admin queue reads.

Adds the ARCHIVE lifecycle enum value and nullable Member archive metadata with a SET NULL FK to MemberLifecycleActionRequest. Old code ignores the new enum value and columns; run during low traffic because Member writes are low volume.

Adds a CHECK constraint enforcing stayStart < stayEnd on BookingGuest. Existing rows were backfilled by 20260524090000_add_booking_guest_stay_ranges; before migrate verify no invalid BookingGuest ranges remain and run during low traffic because BookingGuest writes are capacity-sensitive.
Adds a default-true NotificationPreference flag for admin booking-change-request alerts. Existing admin notification rows backfill through the default value; run during normal low traffic.
Adds a PaymentRecoveryOperationType enum value for booking-modification refunds. Old code ignores the new value; deploy before any runtime enqueues the new operation type.

Renames BookingChangeRequest status values and Member FK columns to match sibling review queues. Treat as a controlled low-traffic migration from the prior booking-change runtime: verify the booking-change request queue is idle, ensure old app colors are drained or blocked from booking-change request traffic, and use the breaking-migration override only with that operator acknowledgement.

Adds nullable BookingChangeRequest.linkedModificationId plus an index and SET NULL FK to BookingModification. Old code ignores the nullable column; run during low traffic because BookingModification is a booking-write table.

Adds triggers enforcing BookingGuest stay ranges inside the parent Booking envelope. Application code already writes envelope-bounded ranges; before migrate verify no existing violations and run during low traffic because Booking and BookingGuest writes are capacity-sensitive.

Renames promo-code cap columns, backfills maxUsesPerMember from singleUse, drops singleUse, and adds nullable/default cap fields. Treat as a controlled promo maintenance migration: verify promo administration and booking promo validation traffic is idle or routed to the new runtime only, and use the breaking-migration override only with that operator acknowledgement.

Booking is hot/write-active; this migration adds nullable soft-delete columns (deletedAt, deletedById, deletedReason) plus Booking indexes and a SET NULL FK from deletedById to Member. Old code ignores the nullable columns; deploy during low booking traffic and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure before cutover.

Creates PromoRedemptionAllocation, backfills one allocation per existing PromoRedemption, recalculates PromoCode.currentRedemptions from allocations, and installs PromoRedemption insert/update triggers so old app colors still create one-booker allocations during blue/green drain. Run during low promo-booking traffic because PromoRedemption and PromoCode are booking-write tables.

Adds AdminReviewStatus and AWAITING_REVIEW enum values plus nullable Booking admin-review columns, SET NULL FK, indexes, and a bounded backfill of existing requiresAdminReview Booking rows. Booking is write-active; deploy during low booking/payment traffic, verify no long Booking or Payment write transactions before migrate, and rely on the deploy guard/lock timeout to stop before cutover if migrate fails. Old code ignores the nullable columns but is not treated as fully enum-compatible with backfilled AWAITING_REVIEW rows.
Adds default-true ClubModuleSettings booleans for bed allocation and Internet Banking activation. Old code ignores the columns; table writes are admin-only and low volume.

Adds FIXED_NIGHTLY_PRICE and FixedNightlyMode plus nullable PromoCode fields, default-zero Booking/PromoRedemption/PromoRedemptionAllocation adjustment columns, and bounded backfills from existing discount cents. Run during low promo-booking traffic, and avoid creating fixed-nightly promos until old app colors have drained.

Creates LodgeRoom, LodgeBed, BedAllocation, BedAllocationSource, indexes, and FKs. Old code ignores the new inventory/allocation tables; run during low booking traffic before enabling bed allocation operations.
Creates the singleton BedAllocationSettings row and updated-by index. Old code ignores the new table; writes are admin-only and low volume.

Adds PaymentSource plus source/reference/Xero invoice fields, drops PaymentTransaction.stripePaymentIntentId NOT NULL, and indexes source/status lookups. Run during low payment traffic and do not enable Internet Banking payments until old app colors have drained.

Adds BOOKING_MODIFICATION_REFUND plus nullable MemberCredit.sourceBookingModificationId and FK/index. Old code ignores the nullable field and enum value until new settlement paths create source-linked credits.

Replaces the nonunique MemberCredit.sourceBookingModificationId index with a unique index so booking-modification credits are idempotent. Verify no duplicate non-null sourceBookingModificationId values before deploy; run during low booking-modification/account-credit traffic.

Adds PromoCode.assignedMembersOnlyOwnNights with a default of true. Old code ignores the column; run during low promo-booking traffic and review assigned-member promo behaviour before enabling scoped promotions.

Creates PromoRedemptionGuestTarget plus indexes and FKs to PromoRedemption, Booking, and BookingGuest. Old code ignores the table; deploy before using guest-night promo targeting and run during low promo-booking traffic.
Creates PageContent with unique slug/path indexes for database-managed public pages. Old code ignores the table; locks are limited to new objects.
Inserts starter PageContent rows with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING for deploy-only environments. Old code ignores the table and existing admin-edited rows are not changed.
Adds nullable Booking.preArrivalReminderSentAt, EmailMessageSetting.doorCode, and a reminder lookup index. Old code ignores the fields; run during low booking traffic because Booking is write-active.
Creates ClubThemeFont and the singleton ClubTheme table with a guarded default row. Old code ignores the table; locks are limited to new objects.
Creates LodgeInstructionKey and LodgeInstruction, then backfills open, close, and day-to-day rows with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Old code ignores the table.
Adds nullable Booking.requestedRoomId plus a SET NULL FK to LodgeRoom and an index. Old code ignores the field; run during low booking traffic and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout.

Adds PromoCode.internal and WorkPartyEvent with date/status indexes and a promo FK. Old code ignores the table and column; run during low promo/admin traffic and create work-party events only after the new runtime is live.
Creates MediaImage for database-backed uploaded images. Old code ignores the table; verify database storage and backup capacity before broad image-manager use.
Adds Booking.cancelIfGuestsBumped with a default of false. Old code ignores the field; run during low booking traffic.

Adds booking request/payment-link enums, tables, settings, notification preference, indexes, and FKs. Old code ignores the new tables; run during normal low traffic and enable public request links after the new runtime is live.
Adds SCHOOL to BookingRequestType and school-specific nullable columns. Old 0.7.x code ignores the BookingRequest table; deploy before accepting school request submissions.
Guardedly updates only the untouched starter home PageContent copy to remove deployment-specific wording. Admin-edited rows are left unchanged.

Adds nullable Booking.parentBookingId plus a SET NULL self-FK and index for linked member/non-member bookings. Old code ignores the field; run during low booking traffic and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout.

Creates BookingGuestNight, backfills one row per existing guest night, and splits BookingGuest.priceCents exactly across nights. Old code ignores the table but does not dual-write new rows, so run during low booking traffic, avoid booking/guest writes during migrate and cutover, and verify active guests have night rows before enabling multi-date range stays.
Adds ClubTheme.rawCss with a default empty string. Old code ignores the field; writes are admin-only and low volume.
Inserts the starter 404 PageContent row with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Old code ignores the row and existing content is not overwritten.
Creates BookingEventType and BookingEvent with indexes plus a restrictive Booking FK. Old code ignores the table; run during low booking traffic and note that historical events are not backfilled.

Drops DB-level defaults from BedAllocationSettings.updatedAt and ClubTheme.updatedAt to match Prisma @updatedAt semantics. Prisma Client sets the values on writes; avoid raw inserts that omit updatedAt after this migration.

Creates lodge induction enums, the checklist template/section/item tables, MemberInduction/MemberInductionItemResult/MemberInductionSignOff, and MembershipNominationSettings, plus adds Member.requiresInduction BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false. The new referencing tables are empty, so FKs to Member and MemberApplication add only a brief lock with no row validation scan; the constant-default ADD COLUMN is metadata-only on PostgreSQL 11+ (brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, no rewrite). Old code ignores the new tables and column; run during low traffic.

Adds nullable MemberInduction.selfAssessedAt and selfAssessmentJson (metadata-only ADD COLUMN on PostgreSQL 11+, no rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on MemberInduction) and creates the MemberInductionAssignedSigner table with unique/composite indexes and FKs to MemberInduction and Member. New table is empty so FK validation adds only brief locks on the referenced tables with no row scan. Old code ignores the new columns and table; run during low induction traffic.

Creates GroupBooking and GroupBookingJoin tables plus group-booking enums, indexes, and nullable relations to Booking and Member. Old code ignores the new tables; run during low booking traffic and do not open shareable group joins until the new runtime is live.

Adds Booking.organiserSettled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false for organiser-pays group child bookings. PostgreSQL 11+ handles the constant default without a table rewrite, but Booking is write-active so run during low booking traffic and avoid organiser-pays joins until old app colours have drained.

Creates GroupBookingSettlement with Stripe PaymentIntent tracking and a FK to GroupBooking. Old code ignores the new table; run before accepting organiser-pays settlement and during low group-booking traffic.

Adds defaulted PaymentSource and nullable Xero invoice fields to GroupBookingSettlement for Internet Banking settlement. Old code ignores the new columns; enable Internet Banking group settlement only after the new runtime is serving traffic.
Creates WhakapapaReportCache with cached JSON payloads, freshness indexes, and freeze-window metadata. Old code ignores the new table; locks are limited to new objects.

Idempotently adds WhakapapaReportCache.frozenUntil and its index for environments that received the earlier cache migration before the freeze column existed. Old code ignores the nullable column; locks are limited to the low-write cache table.

Creates Locker with nullable Member allocation, name and allocation indexes, and a SET NULL FK to Member. Old code ignores the new table; run during low member-admin traffic before enabling locker administration.

Replaces the global ProcessedWebhookEvent.eventId unique index with a composite source/eventId unique index so provider event IDs cannot collide across Stripe, Xero, and SES. Existing rows keep the same values and old code still writes source and eventId; run during low webhook traffic and avoid replay/backfill jobs during the index swap.

Adds nullable XeroToken.refreshInProgressUntil for a database-backed operational Xero refresh lease. Old code ignores the nullable column; run during low Xero activity so token refresh workers are quiet during the brief XeroToken lock.

Checks for exact duplicate Locker.name values, narrows the column to VARCHAR(200), replaces the nonunique name index with a unique index, and leaves the member allocation FK unchanged. Old code can keep reading/writing lockers as long as it does not create duplicate names; run during low admin traffic and resolve any duplicate locker names before migrate.

Adds default-true Admin Modules booleans for group bookings, lockers, induction, work parties, promo codes, hut leaders, communications, and skifield conditions. ClubModuleSettings is low-write and usually single-row; old code ignores the new columns and upgraded installs keep previous enabled behavior.

Adds nullable Member.lifeMemberDate and Member.comments. Old code ignores the nullable fields; Member is hot enough to run during low member-admin traffic and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout.

Idempotently creates Gender and Title enums and adds nullable Member.title and Member.gender. Old code ignores the nullable fields; run during low member-admin traffic and avoid new title/gender-dependent workflows until the new runtime is live.

Adds CHART_OF_ACCOUNTS to FinanceSnapshotType for finance revenue reconciliation. Existing rows are unchanged; do not run the new chart-of-accounts finance sync until the new runtime is serving traffic.
Creates the singleton MembershipLockoutSettings table for subscription booking lockout controls. Old code ignores the new table; locks are limited to new objects.

Adds short-lived ASSOCIATE and LIFE Role enum values, nullable Member.occupation, and the MemberFieldsSettings singleton table. Existing members are not backfilled to new roles; old code remains compatible while no old app colour is asked to read newly assigned role values. A later pre-deployment contract migration collapses the membership-category role values back into USER before live use.

Drops the retired separate finance Xero token store and API-usage metering tables (FinanceXeroToken, FinanceXeroApiUsageDaily, FinanceXeroApiUsageEvent) after the finance dashboard moved onto the single operational Xero connection. Ship the code that stops reading and writing these tables first; no deployed runtime references them (no src/ readers or writers remain), so old app colours stay compatible during the blue/green window. Each DROP TABLE takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on these low-traffic finance-only tables with no row-validation scan; run during low finance-admin traffic.

Changes ClubModuleSettings defaults for the high-risk capability modules (kiosk, chores, finance dashboard, waitlist, Xero integration, bed allocation, and Internet Banking payments) to false and repairs only the untouched singleton default row where updatedByMemberId is null. General-purpose modules stay default-on, explicitly saved admin rows are preserved, and old code keeps reading booleans normally; run during low admin traffic.

Adds booking-request quote enums/table, nullable BookingRequest quote/catering/hold columns, and nullable SET NULL FKs to Booking and BookingRequestQuote. Existing rows are not backfilled and old code ignores the new nullable columns/table; Booking and BookingRequest locks are brief but run during low booking-request traffic and avoid creating public quote links until the new runtime is live.

Adds FinanceReportCategoryKind plus new FinanceReportCategory and FinanceReportCategoryMapping tables for Treasurer-controlled finance dashboard grouping. Old code ignores the new enum and tables; locks are limited to new objects and no existing finance snapshots are rewritten.

Adds BookingMessageOverride and InternetBankingPaymentSettings plus nullable/default Internet Banking hold snapshot fields on Payment. Defaults preserve current behavior: Internet Banking bookings are not held and have no lead-time cutoff. Old code ignores the new tables and Payment columns; Payment is write-active, so run during low payment traffic and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout/failure before cutover.

Adds defaulted NominationToken.reminderCount, nullable lastSentAt, backfills lastSentAt from createdAt, and adds a due-token scan index. Old code ignores the new columns and index; run during low membership-application traffic and avoid relying on automatic nomination reminders until the new runtime is live.

Adds a default-false ClubModuleSettings boolean for Addy address autocomplete. Old code ignores the column, existing admin-saved module rows keep all prior settings, and the low-write singleton table only takes a brief metadata lock; run during low admin traffic.

Creates membership type behavior enums plus MembershipType and SeasonalMembershipAssignment tables, seeds four built-in types, and backfills one current-season assignment per existing Member using role-to-type defaults. Old code ignores the new tables; run during low member-admin traffic because the migration reads Member and writes only the new assignment table with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

Creates CommitteeRole and CommitteeAssignment tables, seeds master roles from existing CommitteeMember rows, and backfills hidden assignments only for exact legacy committee-email to Member.email matches. Existing public CommitteeMember rows are not changed; old code ignores the new tables. Run during low member-admin traffic because the migration reads Member and CommitteeMember and writes only new committee assignment tables with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

Adds AccessRole, MemberAccessRole, membership assignment source, membership-type age-tier eligibility, and Xero contact-group rule tables. Backfills access roles from legacy Member.role and financeAccessLevel, merges legacy RESERVE membership assignments into ASSOCIATE, seeds School/Non-Member/Family types, and copies existing age-tier Xero groups into rule rows. Old code keeps reading Member.role and financeAccessLevel and ignores the new tables; run during low member-admin traffic because the migration reads Member, MembershipType, SeasonalMembershipAssignment, and AgeTierSetting.

Renames the member-facing Member.role compatibility value from MEMBER to USER and removes the one-day ASSOCIATE/LIFE role enum values before any live deployment used them. Existing MEMBER, USER, ASSOCIATE, and LIFE rows keep or receive MemberAccessRole.USER, then Member.role is rewritten to USER before the Role enum is recreated with USER, ADMIN, LODGE, NON_MEMBER, and SCHOOL. Ship only with code that no longer writes MEMBER/ASSOCIATE/LIFE to Member.role; run during low member-admin traffic because Member.role is rewritten.

Adds nullable SeasonalMembershipAssignment.applyFrom DATE for admin-recorded mid-season membership type changeovers. Old code ignores the nullable column and existing rows remain unchanged; run during low member-admin traffic because SeasonalMembershipAssignment is member-admin write-active but the ALTER is metadata-only apart from a brief lock.

Adds nullable CommitteeRole.contactEmail and backfills it from legacy CommitteeMember role aliases where available. Old code ignores the nullable column and still reads existing committee assignment data; run during low admin/contact traffic because CommitteeRole is small and the backfill reads CommitteeMember and updates only existing role rows.

Inserts starter Privacy, Terms, and FAQ PageContent rows with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. Old code ignores the rows while static routes still serve those paths; existing admin-edited rows are not changed.

Creates the Lodge table, seeds one row from EmailMessageSetting identity fields when none exists, and adds ClubModuleSettings.multiLodge with a constant false default. Old code ignores the new table and column; ADD COLUMN with constant default is metadata-only on the single-row ClubModuleSettings table.

Adds nullable lodgeId to LodgeRoom, Locker, Season, Booking, ChoreTemplate, CancellationPolicy, BookingPeriod, MinimumStayPolicy and soft-link lodgeId to the settings singletons, then backfills entity rows to the sole lodge. Old code ignores the nullable columns. Booking is hot: the nullable ADD COLUMN is metadata-only, the FK is added NOT VALID then validated (SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE, writes unblocked), the lodgeId index is small, and the backfill UPDATE takes row locks only on rows where lodgeId IS NULL in one pass; run during low booking traffic. Policy tables keep null lodgeId as their club-wide default state. NOT NULL and unique re-scoping ship later as a contract release.

Adds LodgeSettings.hutLeaderLookaheadDays with a default of 14. LodgeSettings is a singleton low-write admin settings row; old code ignores the column, and new code falls back to 14 if the setting is missing.

Adds HUT_LEADER to InductionKind, nullable/default hut-leader eligibility fields on Member, and per-kind template activation metadata. If legacy data has multiple active templates for a kind, the newest stays active before the partial unique index is added. It also clears only in-flight self-assessment/per-item induction result state that the new single-Pass flow no longer uses; completed historical rows are preserved. Run during low membership-admin/induction traffic.

Adds TwoFactorMethod, Member two-factor columns, ClubModuleSettings.twoFactor default false, and new hashed email-code/recovery-code tables. Old code ignores the new nullable/default columns and new tables; keep the two-factor module disabled until the new runtime is serving traffic, then enable from Admin > Modules.

Adds the LodgeAccessKind enum, the MemberLodgeAccess grant table, and nullable HutLeaderAssignment.lodgeId backfilled to the sole lodge. Old code ignores the new table and nullable column; HutLeaderAssignment is small and admin-write-only so the ADD COLUMN and backfill locks are brief. Booking eligibility stays default-open until rows exist.
Adds the PromoCodeLodge junction for optional per-lodge promo restrictions. No rows means redeemable at every lodge, so existing promos behave identically; old code ignores the new table entirely.

Loosens CancellationPolicy tier uniqueness from global daysBeforeStay to [lodgeId, daysBeforeStay] so per-lodge override tiers can exist (ADR-001 Q3). Existing rows (all null lodgeId, unique days) satisfy the new index before the old one drops. Old code keeps writing null-lodgeId rows with app-side unique days, so it never violates the new index; nulls being distinct means the database no longer hard-enforces club-wide tier uniqueness — the admin route enforces it in a Serializable replace transaction; the null-partition partial unique index is not added (Prisma cannot express a partial index - see docs/multi-lodge/contract-release.md), so club-wide tier uniqueness stays app-enforced.

Adds the WaitlistCrossLodgeOrder enum, BookingDefaults.waitlistCrossLodgeOrder with default OWN_LODGE_FIRST, nullable Booking.waitlistOfferedLodgeId/waitlistOfferedPriceCents, and the BookingWaitlistAlternateLodge opt-in junction (ADR-004). Old code ignores the new columns and table; the enum column ADD with a default is metadata-only on Postgres 11+ and BookingDefaults is a single-row table, so lock impact is negligible. Cross-lodge offers only happen once new code writes junction rows.

Adds nullable WorkPartyEvent.lodgeId with index and SET NULL FK (lodge-scoping audit 2026-07-03). Null keeps today's meaning — a club-wide working bee bookable at any lodge — so existing rows and old code behave identically; only new code writes or enforces the binding. ADD COLUMN nullable is metadata-only and WorkPartyEvent is a tiny admin-written table.

Adds nullable LodgeInstruction.lodgeId with cascade FK and re-scopes key uniqueness from global to [lodgeId, key] (lodge-scoping audit 2026-07-03). Existing rows (all null lodgeId, unique keys) satisfy the new index before the old one drops; old code reads by key across all rows and only ever sees the club-wide null rows it wrote. Admin writes switch from key-upsert to partition-scoped find/create/update; nulls distinct means the club-wide partition is app-guarded until the contract release partial index, matching the cancellation-policy pattern. Tiny admin-written table; lock impact negligible.

Adds nullable BookingRequest.lodgeId with RESTRICT FK (lodge-scoping audit 2026-07-03). Null keeps meaning the club's default lodge (readers resolve request.lodgeId ?? default), so existing rows and old code behave identically; new requests set it from the public form's lodge choice. Nullable ADD COLUMN is metadata-only; BookingRequest is low-write.

Re-scopes LodgeRoom and Locker name uniqueness from global to [lodgeId, name] so each lodge names its own rooms and lockers (pulled forward from the contract release; the reporter hit "Room 1 already exists" seeding a second lodge). Existing rows are globally unique so they satisfy the composite before the old indexes drop. Old code keeps writing lodge-stamped rows and its own club-wide pre-checks, so it never attempts the cross-lodge duplicates the new index now allows; nulls-distinct means null-lodge rows lose DB-level uniqueness, so app checks treat null rows as clashing at every lodge until the contract release enforces NOT NULL. Index swap on small admin-written tables; lock impact negligible.

Guardedly rewraps only the untouched starter FAQ PageContent row from flat h3/p pairs into native details/summary accordion markup. Admin-edited rows are left unchanged; old code renders the same sanitised HTML as plain content.

Adds a default-false ClubModuleSettings boolean for consent-gated Google Analytics. Old code ignores the column, existing admin-saved module rows keep prior settings, and analytics remains disabled until a new runtime, measurement id, admin opt-in, and visitor consent are all present.

Guardedly updates only the untouched starter Privacy PageContent row to describe optional consent-gated GA4. Admin-edited privacy pages are left unchanged; old code renders the same sanitised HTML as plain content.

Adds new AccessRole enum values for read-only admin, booking officer, membership officer, and content manager bundles. Existing rows are unchanged and full admin remains AccessRole.ADMIN. Do not assign the new enum values until the new runtime is serving traffic, because old Prisma clients may not understand newly written enum values.

Creates the empty TwoFactorSessionChallenge table with a foreign key to Member(id). The FK takes a brief lock on Member to validate the constraint but scans and rewrites no existing rows (new table only). Old code ignores the new table; run during low membership-admin traffic.

Adds nullable LodgeSettings.schoolGroupSoftCap so the school-group soft cap (a warning threshold, previously the hardcoded SCHOOL_GROUP_SOFT_CAP constant) becomes per-lodge configurable. Null falls back to the code default, so existing rows and old code behave identically; nullable ADD COLUMN is metadata-only and LodgeSettings is a tiny admin-written table.

Drops and recreates the two BookingGuest/Booking stay-range envelope triggers as DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED constraint triggers. Takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on Booking and BookingGuest to swap the triggers but scans and rewrites no rows. Old code stays compatible because the same cross-table invariant is now checked at COMMIT instead of per-row. Run during low booking-write traffic.

Creates the AccessRoleDefinition table, seeds the six editable default bundles with matrices identical to the legacy hardcoded ones, makes MemberAccessRole.role nullable, and adds a roleDefinitionId FK backfilled for existing enum rows. Old code keeps reading the role enum and behaves identically. Do not create or assign custom (NULL-role) definitions until the new runtime is fully serving traffic, because old Prisma clients select role as non-nullable and can error on NULL-role rows.

Adds default-true BookingDefaults and BookingPeriod booleans for member-priority non-member holds, preserving existing Members First behavior until an admin disables it. Guardedly refreshes only untouched starter Terms/FAQ PageContent copy; admin-edited rows are left unchanged. Old code ignores the new columns and continues treating holds as enabled.

Enforces NOT NULL on lodgeId for LodgeRoom, Locker, Season, ChoreTemplate, HutLeaderAssignment, and Booking. old_code_compatible via a default_lodge_id() column DEFAULT set in the SAME migration: an old (pre-lodge) colour's INSERT omits lodgeId and receives the default lodge, so no null is written and NOT NULL holds throughout a blue/green cutover - no outage, no migration abort. The default is kept permanently (harmless; new code always stamps lodgeId explicitly and it only fires for an old colour's omitted-column writes). Booking is hot but the column is fully populated (expand backfill + writer-stamping), so SET NOT NULL is a validation scan, not a rewrite; take the brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock during low booking traffic. The validator recognises the SET NOT NULL + same-column SET DEFAULT pattern as old-code-compatible, so this deploys without ALLOW_BREAKING_BLUE_GREEN_MIGRATIONS.

Single UPDATE flipping ADULT organisation-type members (legacy SCHOOL role or ORG access role) to the new NOT_APPLICABLE AgeTier value; touches only those rows (typically a handful per club) with brief row locks and no DDL. CAUTION: pre-#1440 Prisma clients cannot deserialize NOT_APPLICABLE, so old-color reads of the flipped rows (admin members list, that member's detail, school flows) can error between migrate and cutover — deploy in a quiet window and cut over promptly, or defer this migration until the old color drains (the UPDATE is idempotent and safe to run late).

Two idempotent single-row UPDATEs repairing the 20260708000000 lodge seed's createdAt/updatedAt, which were written with session-time CURRENT_TIMESTAMP and render NZ local time (~12-13h ahead of UTC) on non-UTC databases (the shipped compose sets PGTZ=Pacific/Auckland), letting a same-day app-created lodge sort earlier and silently become the club default (#1627). Clamps a future-skewed seed timestamp to UTC now, then restores the seeded lodge (slug 'lodge') as the strictly-earliest row when another lodge sorts at-or-before it. No-op on UTC databases and single-lodge installs; touches at most one row; old code unaffected (nothing reads absolute createdAt values - only the relative ordering both colours already expect).

Drops the retired MemberInductionItemResult table and the InductionItemResultValue enum after the single-Pass induction flow stopped recording per-item results. Retired at the code level by 20260702100000_induction_workflow_types; no deployed runtime reads or writes this table (no src/ readers or writers remain), so old app colours stay compatible during the blue/green window. Production holds zero rows (verified 2026-07-08, evidence on #1532), so the drop loses no data. DROP TABLE takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on this empty, unreferenced leaf table with no row-validation scan, then DROP TYPE removes the now-unused enum; MemberInduction* are low-write induction tables, not hot tables. Run during low induction/membership-admin traffic.

Drops the retired MemberInduction.selfAssessedAt and selfAssessmentJson columns. 20260702100000_induction_workflow_types NULLed all in-flight values and removed every app-level read/write, and the production retention check on 2026-07-08 confirmed zero non-NULL values across all 4 rows (evidence on #1532), so no data is lost and DROP COLUMN is a metadata-only catalog update (no rewrite, no row scan, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock; MemberInduction is not a hot table). RESIDUAL WINDOW (owner-accepted 2026-07-08, single-release disposition per #1533): the previously deployed colour's Prisma client still names both columns in its default SELECT/RETURNING lists, so between migrate and cutover the old colour errors on the default-selection induction paths (induction.ts create/update RETURNING and findUnique/findFirst/findMany includes, plus the admin inductions list) - reads AND create/update writes on inductions fail with column-does-not-exist until cutover; paths with explicit selects and counts are unaffected. Induction traffic is near-nil (4 rows total in production), so deploy in a quiet window, cut over promptly (or drain the old colour first), and use ALLOW_BREAKING_BLUE_GREEN_MIGRATIONS=1 with that acknowledgement as the override reason - same pattern as the 20260526120000 and 20260708220300 rows.

Contract drop of the value-dead sectionLabel/lineLabel columns and their composite index on FinanceReportCategoryMapping. previous_expand_release names 20260629000000_add_finance_report_category_subtype, the RUNTIME release (PR #938) that removed text-label P&L fallback matching so the app logic has ignored these columns VALUES since; policy prose permits naming the runtime release. OLD-CODE CAVEAT: the columns stayed in the Prisma model until THIS release and Prisma emits explicit column lists, so the previously-deployed colour client still SELECTs sectionLabel/lineLabel by name -- between migrate and cutover its finance-dashboard and mappings-admin READ queries (listFinanceReportCategories: findMany include mappings) error with column-does-not-exist on the draining old colour. WRITES are unaffected (createMany inserts accountCode only, no RETURNING of dropped columns). Impact is admin-only, read-only, brief and retryable. old_code_compatible=yes is asserted in the promo-redesign sense (20260526120000): deploy only with old traffic idle or routed to the new runtime, and use the ALLOW_BREAKING override only with that operator acknowledgement (prefer a low-traffic window). Not a hot table (absent from HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX; neither table nor index name matches); DROP COLUMN is a metadata-only catalog change taking a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, and the preceding DROP INDEX likewise. Zero data loss: read-only production count on #1532 found 31 rows with no non-NULL label values.

Expand-only CREATE TABLE for MemberCreditNoteAllocation (per-note applied-credit allocation lots, #1620) plus its indexes and unique key; no existing table or column is touched. Flagged by the hot-table regex only because the new table declares a single FK REFERENCES "MemberCredit" (appliedToBookingId is a bare scalar with no Booking FK, by design) - creating an empty table with one FK takes only a brief ROW SHARE lock on the referenced table with no validation scan (zero rows) and no rewrite. Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed client has no model for this table and never reads or writes it; only the new APPLIED_CREDIT_ALLOCATION engine references it. No backfill: pre-existing floating credit notes correctly read as fully unallocated (zero rows = full remaining balance by construction). Safe at any traffic level.

Expand-only ADD COLUMN of the nullable MemberCredit.restoredFromBookingId dedupe key plus its unique index (#1636). The nullable ADD COLUMN (no default) is a metadata-only catalog change taking a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock with no table rewrite and no row scan. CREATE UNIQUE INDEX scans MemberCredit to build the index, but the column is brand-new and all-NULL so no uniqueness conflict is possible and the build is fast; MemberCredit is a low/medium-write finance-history table, not a hot booking-path table. Flagged by the hot-table regex only because ALTER TABLE / the ON clause name "MemberCredit". Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed Prisma client has no model field for this column and never reads or writes it (only restoreCreditFromBooking sets it, in the new colour); nullable + all-NULL existing rows satisfy the unique index during the migrate->cutover window. No backfill (existing restore rows stay NULL; their only re-restore vector, the orphaned-applied-credit backfill, is already blocked by its CANCELLATION_REFUND predicate). Safe at any traffic level; prefer a low-write window for the index build on very large ledgers.

Adds raw-SQL partial unique indexes over the club-wide (lodgeId IS NULL) partitions of CancellationPolicy (daysBeforeStay) and LodgeInstruction (key), closing the multi-lodge contract-release deferral: PostgreSQL treats NULLs as distinct under the composite [lodgeId, ...] uniques, so these partitions were app-enforced only. Neither table is hot and no breaking SQL is present; this row exists for documentation, like the multi_lodge_entity_lodge_id_not_null row. A defensive dedupe DELETE precedes each index build (keep greatest updatedAt then id per duplicate group) so a lost-race duplicate can never abort the deploy; app enforcement (Serializable replace transaction / findFirst-then-write) should make it a no-op, and any row it removes is an invisible-duplicate bug state, not user-visible data. Both are small config tables, so the DELETE self-join scan and CREATE UNIQUE INDEX build are brief (brief SHARE lock on writes during each index build; reads unaffected). Old-colour compatibility is total: old code enforces the same uniqueness app-side, so the indexes reject nothing an old colour legitimately writes. Drift-gate note: prisma migrate diff does not surface partial indexes (Member_email_primary_unique / Member_email_login_unique / XeroSyncOperation precedents), so db:check-drift stays green.

Contract drop of the EmailMessageSetting legacy lodge-identity columns (lodgeName, lodgeTravelNote, doorCode) after this same release refactors email identity resolution to always read from Lodge (default lodge when no lodgeId is in scope), retiring syncSoleActiveLodgeIdentity. Single-release disposition per the 20260708220100/20260708220200/20260708220300 precedent. OLD-CODE CAVEAT: the previously deployed colour's Prisma client still names the three columns in its default SELECT/RETURNING lists on the singleton, so between migrate and cutover: member-facing email sends DEGRADE but do not fail (loadPersistedEmailMessageSettings catches the error and falls back to config defaults, and per-booking lodge identity already reads from Lodge on that colour), while the admin email-settings GET/PUT and the lodge-admin create/update routes (whose transactions call the retired sync upsert) error with column-does-not-exist until cutover - admin-only, brief, retryable. Zero data loss: the migration first backfills the default lodge's travelNote/doorCode from the singleton where the Lodge values are NULL (default_lodge_id() as replaced by 20260709120000: isDefault flag first, else oldest active, else oldest - matching the app's getDefaultLodgeId and this release's email resolveLodgeIdentity); Lodge.name is NOT NULL and authoritative so a divergent email-only lodgeName is superseded by design. EmailMessageSetting is a single-row config table (not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX); the backfill touches at most one Lodge row and DROP COLUMN is a metadata-only catalog change with a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock. Deploy in a quiet window with old traffic idle or drained and use ALLOW_BREAKING_BLUE_GREEN_MIGRATIONS=1 with that acknowledgement as the override reason.

Idempotent data repair (#1665): four action-scoped UPDATEs rewrite plaintext door codes in historical AuditLog metadata to the '[set]' redaction convention that PR #1663 established going forward. Covered: LODGE_CREATED/LODGE_UPDATED/LODGE_ACTIVATED/LODGE_DEACTIVATED newLodge/previousLodge.doorCode (the [id] route emits all three update-family actions with the same metadata shape, unredacted from the multi-lodge release until #1663), plus EMAIL_MESSAGE_SETTINGS_UPDATED previousSettings/newSettings.doorCode purely defensively (its redactor shipped in the same commit as the field, so plaintext rows are not expected; the statements are shape-guarded no-ops). The columns themselves were dropped by 20260709130000; this touches only audit history. Shape-guarded jsonb_set: only JSON string values other than '[set]' change, JSON nulls keep recording that no code was set, and re-running is a no-op. No data loss beyond the deliberate secret removal; whether a code existed remains recorded. No DDL; AuditLog is not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX; the UPDATEs use the indexed action column and take brief row locks on matching admin-audit rows only. Old-colour compatibility is total (both colours only read metadata for display; the value shape stays a JSON string). Safe at any traffic level; this row exists for documentation like the 20260708220000 metadata-only precedent.

Expand-only CREATE TABLE of the brand-new PartnerInviteToken (#1682) plus its indexes and two FKs. All DDL targets the new, empty table: the CREATE INDEX builds scan zero rows, and the ALTER TABLE ... ADD CONSTRAINT ... REFERENCES statements take only a brief ROW SHARE lock on the referenced FamilyGroup and Member tables (no validation scan is needed because the referencing table is empty) — flagged by the hot-table regex solely because those two referenced names appear in the FK clauses; neither hot table is rewritten, scanned, or row-locked. Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed Prisma client has no PartnerInviteToken model and never reads or writes the table, and no existing table changes shape. No backfill. Safe at any traffic level.

Expand-only migration for the declared Partner/Husband/Wife relationship (#1742): CREATE TABLE MemberPartnerLink (canonical-pair CHECK, pair unique, five indexes, two raw partial unique indexes for the one-CONFIRMED-partner-per-member invariant — documented in prisma/partial-unique-indexes.tsv) plus ALTER TABLE PartnerInviteToken ADD COLUMN createPartnerLink BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false. All MemberPartnerLink DDL targets the brand-new empty table; its five Member FKs take only a brief ROW SHARE lock on Member (no validation scan — the referencing table is empty), matched by the hot-table regex solely because Member appears in the FK clauses. The PartnerInviteToken ADD COLUMN with a constant default is a PG16 catalog-only change (no rewrite) on a tiny, cold table, and old code ignores the new column (single-use claim guards are updateMany-based, unaffected). Old-colour compatibility is total: the previous Prisma client has no MemberPartnerLink model and its PartnerInviteToken selects name only pre-existing columns. No backfill. Safe at any traffic level.

Expand-only ADD COLUMN of the nullable Booking.adminCapacityHoldAt / adminCapacityHoldByMemberId admin-hold fields (#1764) plus an index and a SET NULL FK to Member. The two nullable ADD COLUMNs (no default) are metadata-only catalog changes taking a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock with no table rewrite and no row scan. CREATE INDEX scans Booking once to build over a brand-new all-NULL column (brief SHARE lock on writes; reads unaffected; the scan is fast because it writes no index entries for NULLs beyond bookkeeping). ADD CONSTRAINT ... REFERENCES Member validates existing Booking rows, but every row is NULL in the new column so the validation scan matches nothing and completes quickly (SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE on Booking, ROW SHARE on Member, both brief). Flagged by the hot-table regex because ALTER TABLE names Booking and the FK names Member. Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed Prisma client has no model fields for these columns and never reads or writes them; only the new colour's admin hold/unhold routes and capacity filter touch them, and nullable + all-NULL rows satisfy both the index and the FK during the migrate->cutover window. No backfill (no booking is admin-held before the feature ships). Safe at any traffic level; prefer a low-write window for the index build on very large Booking tables.

Expand-only ADD COLUMN of the nullable Booking.capacityOverriddenAt / capacityOverriddenByMemberId persisted-override fields (#1771) plus an index and a SET NULL FK to Member. The two nullable ADD COLUMNs (no default) are metadata-only catalog changes taking a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock with no table rewrite and no row scan. CREATE INDEX scans Booking once to build over a brand-new all-NULL column (brief SHARE lock on writes; reads unaffected; the scan is fast because it writes no index entries for NULLs beyond bookkeeping). ADD CONSTRAINT ... REFERENCES Member validates existing Booking rows, but every row is NULL in the new column so the validation scan matches nothing and completes quickly (SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE on Booking, ROW SHARE on Member, both brief). Flagged by the hot-table regex because ALTER TABLE names Booking and the FK names Member. Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed Prisma client has no model fields for these columns and never reads or writes them; only the new colour over-capacity admission sites and payment-time re-checks touch them, and nullable + all-NULL rows satisfy both the index and the FK during the migrate->cutover window. No backfill (no booking carries a persisted override before the feature ships). Safe at any traffic level; prefer a low-write window for the index build on very large Booking tables.

Idempotent data-only correction (#1832) of the singleton default ClubTheme row from the legacy generic sage palette to the current generic teal defaults. The single UPDATE is guarded by id='default', completedAt IS NULL, and an exact match on every stored legacy theme value after the earlier #1244 gold bump (all seven colours, both fonts, NULL logo, and empty raw CSS), so completed, partially customized, Tokoroa, and non-default themes are untouched. ClubTheme is a cold singleton settings table; the statement takes at most one brief row lock and performs no DDL. Old and new colours render both palettes, so blue/green compatibility is total and the migration is safe at any traffic level.

Consolidates the six never-shipped lobby-display feature-branch migrations (schema, name granularity, device templateKey, notice, authoring v2, poll seconds) into ONE expand migration, per ADR-003 and issue #86 (LTV-040) which re-layers the branch onto clean history before the single upstream PR. Purely additive: creates the DisplayLayout, DisplayTemplate and LodgeDisplayDevice tables plus three nullable Lodge columns (displayConfig, displayNameGranularity, displayNotice) and the flagged-off ClubModuleSettings.lobbyDisplay boolean. The boolean is an ADD COLUMN with a constant DEFAULT false, which is a metadata-only catalog change on modern Postgres (no table rewrite, no row scan); the nullable columns and CREATE TABLEs are likewise metadata-only, taking only brief locks on empty, unreferenced objects. DisplayLayout/DisplayTemplate/LodgeDisplayDevice are absent from HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX and Lodge/ClubModuleSettings gain only additive columns. old_code_compatible=yes: the previously deployed colour has no Prisma model field for any of these tables, columns or the boolean and never reads or writes them (the lobbyDisplay module flag defaults OFF and no production club has enabled it, so the feature never shipped). Any staging/dev database that applied the superseded migrations must be reset with a fresh migrate deploy — the documented ADR-003 clean-slate plan for this unshipped branch; this migration never runs against a production blue/green window. Safe at any traffic level.

Expand-only additive ADD COLUMN of Member.lodgeScreenPhoneOptIn and Lodge.showGuestPhonesOnScreens (both BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false), the two-sided phone-visibility opt-in for lodge screens (#37/#124). ADD COLUMN with a constant default is a metadata-only catalog change on modern Postgres (no table rewrite, no row scan), taking a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock. Flagged by the hot-table regex only because ALTER TABLE names Member; no rewrite/scan occurs. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no field for either column and never reads or writes them, and both default false preserves current behaviour (no phones shown). No backfill. Enforcement is deferred to the serialisers (#125). Safe at any traffic level.

Additive fee-authority migration (#1855): creates two new schedule tables/enums and btree_gist exclusion constraints; adds nullable public-copy/listing fields and FamilyGroup.billingMembershipId, a Prisma-representable SET NULL FK to the selected FamilyGroupMember row. The API accepts a member ID only after resolving an active, unarchived membership in the same family; deleting that join row clears the recipient automatically. The public flag has a constant false default (PG16 catalog-only); the nullable FamilyGroup column is metadata-only. Index/FK creation briefly locks the cold FamilyGroup/FamilyGroupMember configuration tables and validates only an all-NULL new column, so no existing-row failure is possible. Entrance backfill reads small Xero mapping tables only, validates legacy text/range before integer cast, writes explicit UTC updatedAt values, and derives the effective date from statement time in Pacific/Auckland. Old code ignores all additions and continues using retained mapping amounts; new code is schedule-first with one-release fallback. No provider calls, destructive SQL, hot-row rewrite, or old-colour incompatibility.

Additive subscription-billing migration (#1857): creates new enums and cold workflow/settings/snapshot/coverage/exception tables only. Existing Member and MemberSubscription rows are not rewritten; no existing table receives a required column. Foreign keys point from initially empty tables to retained records and therefore cannot fail validation on existing data. Unique indexes and checks protect immutable charge identity, one charge per subscription, one charge per family/membership-type/year, integer-cent/non-negative amounts, inclusive-month bounds, due-day bounds, and zero-cent NO_INVOICE snapshots; nullable account/item columns freeze provider mapping only on new invoice charges. Old code ignores all additions while new code uses the durable tables. No provider calls, destructive SQL, hot-table rewrite, or old-colour incompatibility.

Expand-only additive migration for the exclusive whole-lodge hold (#116/#117, ADR-001): ADD COLUMN Booking.wholeLodgeHold (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false), Booking.wholeLodgeHoldAt (nullable), Booking.wholeLodgeHoldByMemberId (nullable), and BookingRequest.exclusivityRequested (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false). ADD COLUMN with a constant default is a metadata-only catalog change on modern Postgres (no table rewrite, no row scan), a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock; the nullable columns are likewise metadata-only. Adds a plain (non-CONCURRENT) index Booking_wholeLodgeHoldByMemberId_idx and an FK to Member(id) ON DELETE SET NULL: the index build takes a SHARE lock blocking Booking writes for its (all-null, fast) build, and ADD CONSTRAINT validates existing rows (all null, trivially valid) under a brief lock — both acceptable at this table's size; switch to CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY if Booking grows large. Flagged by the hot-table regex only because ALTER TABLE names Booking. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no fields for these columns and never reads or writes them, and every default preserves current behaviour (no exclusive holds). No backfill. Capacity enforcement is deferred to #118.

Additive public-content visibility migration (#1856): creates one cold singleton settings table only, with five boolean gates defaulting false. No existing table or row is altered, and old code ignores the new table. New code treats a missing singleton exactly like all-false, so mixed blue/green colours remain fail-closed and compatible before and after migration. The empty-table CREATE and primary-key index take no hot-row locks, perform no backfill or provider call, and are safe at any traffic level.

Contract drop of the legacy standalone public CommitteeMember directory table (added 20260410020000) after the member-linked committee system (CommitteeRole + CommitteeAssignment, added 20260629130000_add_committee_roles_assignments) superseded it: the public committee (/api/committee, the {{committee-members-cards}} embed) and contact recipient routing read only from published active assignments, and this release retires the last reader/writer, the admin committee CRUD. Single-release disposition per the 20260708220100/20260708220200/20260708220300 precedent. OLD-CODE CAVEAT: because the admin CRUD only retires in THIS release, the previously deployed colour still models CommitteeMember and its admin committee GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE routes read and write the table, so between migrate and cutover those admin-only routes error with relation-does-not-exist until cutover; the public committee and contact surfaces already read from assignments on both colours and are unaffected. old_code_compatible=yes is asserted in the promo-redesign sense (20260526120000): deploy only with old traffic idle or routed to the new runtime and use ALLOW_BREAKING_BLUE_GREEN_MIGRATIONS=1 with that operator acknowledgement (prefer a low admin-traffic window). Zero data loss beyond the retired directory: the role/assignment backfill ran while the table existed (20260629130000, 20260701130000) and no assignment or contact data lives only in CommitteeMember. Free-standing table with no foreign keys in or out (not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX); DROP TABLE without CASCADE takes a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on this low-write admin-only table with no row-validation scan, and the CommitteeMember_contactKey_key unique index drops with it. The migration file's in-file comment "Drop after cutover" predates the current pipeline — migrations run at deploy step 13, before cutover at step 16 — and this ledger/docs deployment order is authoritative.

Additive family-billing-mode migration (#159): CREATE TYPE FamilyBillingMode and ALTER TABLE MembershipSubscriptionBillingSettings ADD COLUMN familyBillingMode with a constant default of BILL_FAMILY_VIA_BILLING_MEMBER. The ADD COLUMN with a constant default is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) on the single-row (id='default') settings table, and CREATE TYPE only registers a new enum. MembershipSubscriptionBillingSettings is a cold admin-only singleton and is not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX. Old-colour compatibility is total: the previously deployed Prisma client has no model field for the new column and never reads or writes it; only the new colour's subscription-billing settings surface and billing/fee-config guards consult it, and the default preserves today's family-billing behaviour for every existing deployment during the migrate->cutover window. No backfill and no provider call. Safe at any traffic level.

Additive expand-only webhook-dedup lease migration (F16, #1887): ALTER TABLE ProcessedWebhookEvent ADD COLUMN status (TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'PROCESSING') and processingStartedAt (TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP). Both column defaults are constant/STABLE, so each ADD COLUMN is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock). The one-time backfill sets status='COMPLETED' only for rows before the explicit UTC release cutoff 2026-07-15 00:00:00; the fixed cutoff avoids session-clock-dependent DML and deliberately leaves newer rows at PROCESSING for safe idempotent reprocessing on redelivery. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no status/processingStartedAt fields and never reads or writes them; its inserts during the migrate->cutover window land at the PROCESSING default and are safely reprocessed by the new colour on redelivery rather than ACKed as done, so no event is dropped. New colour sets PROCESSING explicitly and flips to COMPLETED on success. No provider calls, no destructive SQL, no foreign-key or index changes. The table has no pruning job, so the bounded historical backfill may scan its history once but never rewrites newer rows or repeats.

Expand-only additive migration for manual mark-paid provenance (E14, #1944): ALTER TABLE MemberSubscription ADD COLUMN manuallyMarkedPaidAt (nullable TIMESTAMP), manuallyMarkedPaidByMemberId (nullable TEXT), manualPaymentNote (nullable VARCHAR(500)). Nullable ADD COLUMN with no default is a metadata-only catalog change on modern Postgres (no table rewrite, no row scan), a brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock. Adds a plain (non-CONCURRENT) index MemberSubscription_manuallyMarkedPaidByMemberId_idx and an FK to Member(id) ON DELETE SET NULL: the index build takes a SHARE lock blocking MemberSubscription writes for its (all-NULL, fast) build, and ADD CONSTRAINT validates existing rows (all NULL, trivially valid) under a brief lock — both acceptable at this table's size (one row per member per season); switch to CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY if it grows large. Flagged by the hot-table regex because ALTER TABLE names MemberSubscription and the FK REFERENCES Member. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no fields for these columns and never reads or writes them; all columns are nullable so existing writers are unaffected, and NULL provenance preserves current behaviour (no manual payments recorded). No backfill.

Additive pricing re-key migration (#1930, E4): ADD COLUMN MembershipType.ageGroupsApply (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true), BookingGuest.rateMembershipTypeId (nullable TEXT), XeroItemCodeMapping.membershipTypeId (nullable TEXT), and GroupDiscountSetting.rateMembershipTypeId (nullable TEXT); CREATE TABLE MembershipTypeSeasonRate; new btree + raw-SQL partial unique indexes; and a one-time fan-out backfill. Every ADD COLUMN uses a constant/NULL default, so each is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) even on hot BookingGuest — flagged by the hot-table regex only because ALTER TABLE names BookingGuest; no rewrite or row scan occurs. The new MembershipTypeSeasonRate table and its indexes build empty then take the backfill INSERTs. New unique/partial indexes on the cold XeroItemCodeMapping config table validate only rows whose membershipTypeId is NULL (all pre-existing rows), so they are trivially distinct and cannot fail. The fan-out backfill INSERTs copy SeasonRate/XeroItemCodeMapping HUT_FEE rows into the new key (isMember=true -> every MEMBER_RATE type, isMember=false -> NON_MEMBER) and set GroupDiscountSetting.rateMembershipTypeId to the FULL type id; it reads small config tables only, writes literal constant UTC timestamps ('2026-07-16 00:00:00') for createdAt/updatedAt, uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on the HUT_FEE fan-out (legacy NULL-ageTier duplicate rows cannot abort it; oldest row wins), and performs no provider call or destructive SQL. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no field for any new column or the new table and never reads or writes them; the legacy boolean-keyed SeasonRate table and XeroItemCodeMapping.isMember column are RETAINED and still price/invoice on the old colour, so both colours resolve identical day-one prices and item codes during migrate->cutover. New code prices from MembershipTypeSeasonRate and reads the BookingGuest snapshot (NULL falls back isMember -> FULL/NON_MEMBER). No destructive SQL, no hot-row rewrite, no old-colour incompatibility; the retained structures drop in the deferred E13.

Xero member-grouping cutover (E8, #1934): creates the XeroMemberGroupingMode enum and the cold singleton XeroGroupingSettings table, then converges grouping config on the small admin-only XeroContactGroupRule table — a first-run-only UPDATE deactivates every pre-existing rule the migration did not backfill itself (fenced inside a DO-block on the settings singleton not existing, so re-runs never deactivate rules admins create via the new UI), a defensive DELETE dedupes rule shapes (earliest createdAt/id wins, NULLs comparing equal) before the NULLS NOT DISTINCT unique index builds, and INSERT ... SELECT backfills copy AgeTierSetting/AgeTierXeroAcceptedContactGroup group config into tier-only rules with deterministic ids and ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. All INSERT/UPDATE payloads write explicit UTC via timezone('UTC', statement_timestamp()) — no session-clock CURRENT_TIMESTAMP/now() DML (#1627/#1656 gate); the only CURRENT_TIMESTAMP is a DDL column DEFAULT. No hot table is touched (none of these tables is in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX) and there is no destructive SQL; the UPDATE/DELETE take brief row locks on a config table that is typically tens of rows, and the unique-index build over it is near-instant. Performs ZERO Xero calls: only local config rows change, and no member is re-grouped until a later trigger or the runbook's admin-run dry-run + bulk re-sync. OLD-CODE CAVEAT (window-bounded, admin-only): the table keeps its exact shape, so the previously deployed colour keeps working — its live sync never read XeroContactGroupRule, and its membership-types editor still lists/saves rules; a rule save on the draining old colour rewrites that type's rows (delete + recreate, potentially isActive=true again), which the old colour still ignores but the new colour would treat as live config. Old-editor rows are always type-keyed and app-deduped, so they cannot violate the new unique index. Deploy in a quiet admin window, cut over promptly, and re-verify runbook step 2 (only tier-only backfill rules active) before any bulk re-sync. Safe at any traffic level otherwise; this row exists for documentation like the 20260709000100 precedent.

Additive joining-fee re-key migration (#1931, E5): CREATE TABLE JoiningFee (keyed by membershipTypeId x optional AgeTier, integer-cent CHECK, date-order CHECK), its btree unique + effective-lookup indexes and one raw-SQL partial unique (WHERE "ageTier" IS NULL, recorded in prisma/partial-unique-indexes.tsv), a RESTRICT FK to the cold MembershipType config table, a one-time fan-out backfill, and an UPDATE re-keying the ENTRANCE_FEE XeroItemCodeMapping rows to JOINING_FEE. The new JoiningFee table and its indexes build empty then take the backfill INSERTs. The fan-out reads only the small EntranceFee/XeroItemCodeMapping/XeroAccountMapping config tables, copies every EntranceFee window verbatim (plus a materialised open window from any legacy mapping amount for a category lacking a schedule row) into JoiningFee — ADULT/YOUTH/CHILD(+INFANT fold) tiers of every liable membership type, FAMILY amount to the built-in Family type as a flat NULL-tier row — writes literal UTC timestamps via timezone(UTC, statement_timestamp()) for createdAt/updatedAt (the only session-derived value is an honest Pacific/Auckland boundary date for a materialised legacy window, matching the 20260713110000 precedent), uses ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, and performs no provider call or destructive SQL. The EntranceFee table and XeroItemCodeMapping amounts are RETAINED (E13 drops EntranceFee after a soak); the amount source is copied, not moved. None of JoiningFee/MembershipType/XeroItemCodeMapping/XeroAccountMapping is in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX. OLD-CODE CAVEAT (window-bounded, MONEY-AFFECTING): the EntranceFee schedule table is NEW in this release, so the previously deployed colour never reads it; once this UPDATE re-keys the ENTRANCE_FEE XeroItemCodeMapping rows to JOINING_FEE, the OLD colour resolves BOTH the item code AND the amount of a new entrance-fee invoice from the legacy flat entranceFeeItem/entranceFeeAmountCents mappings — during the migrate->cutover window it can mint a wrong per-category amount, or (if the flat amount is unset) mark the operation SUCCEEDED/skipped so the invoice is silently never created. Operations queued BEFORE the window carry frozen amount/item payloads and replay safely. New code reads JoiningFee for amounts and JOINING_FEE item codes. Membership approvals and entrance-fee minting must be fully idle on the OLD colour from migrate until cutover. No hot-row rewrite; safe at any traffic level otherwise.

Additive annual-fee-components + billing-family migration (#1932, E6): CREATE TABLE MembershipAnnualFeeComponent (CASCADE FK to the cold MembershipAnnualFee config table) and MembershipSubscriptionChargeComponent (RESTRICT FK to MembershipSubscriptionCharge), their sortOrder btree indexes, ADD COLUMN Member.billingFamilyGroupId (nullable TEXT) with a btree index and a SET NULL FK to FamilyGroup, and two one-time additive backfills. The nullable ADD COLUMN takes no default so it is a PostgreSQL catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) even though ALTER TABLE names the hot Member table — no rewrite or row scan occurs, and the new index is a plain (non-CONCURRENT) build over a nullable column that is NULL for every existing row — fast, but briefly blocking writes to Member while it builds. Both new component tables build empty then take the backfill INSERTs. Backfill 1 inserts one default component per existing non-NO_INVOICE MembershipAnnualFee (label Annual membership fee, amount = fee.amountCents, prorate true). Backfill 2 inserts one frozen snapshot component per existing invoiceable MembershipSubscriptionCharge (billingBasis <> NO_INVOICE AND xeroAccountCode IS NOT NULL) derived VERBATIM from the charge frozen columns, description rebuilt from the exact historical invoice-build template incl. pluralization; both backfills read only these tables, write gen_random_uuid ids and constant timezone('UTC', statement_timestamp()), and perform no provider call or destructive SQL. No existing charge, invoice, or amount is mutated (owner-approved additive derivation, 2026-07-16). Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no field for either new table or the new column and never reads or writes them; the annual-fee total stays authoritative so the old colour continues to mint the identical single-line invoice during migrate->cutover, and the new colour reads components to emit one line per component (a backfilled legacy charge reproduces the identical single line). No destructive SQL, no hot-row rewrite. Safe at any traffic level.

Additive public-content visibility gate (#1933, E7): ALTER TABLE PublicContentSettings ADD COLUMN annualFees (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false), the double-opt-in flag for the new {{annual-fees}} public embed. A nullable-with-constant-default ADD COLUMN is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, no row scan, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) on the cold singleton PublicContentSettings table (one row, id=default); the table is not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX. Two back-compat backfills follow (E7 review, Findings F1/F2), each a small UPDATE on a cold, non-HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX config table with no session clock and no provider call: (F2) UPDATE PublicContentSettings SET annualFees = membershipTypes seeds the new {{membership-types}}/{{annual-fees}} gate from the retired legacy membershipTypes toggle so a previously-visible embed stays visible (one-row singleton; legacy column retained, not dropped); (F1) UPDATE MembershipType SET publiclyListed = true WHERE key IN ('FULL','FAMILY') AND publiclyListed = false restores the E6 unconditional FULL/FAMILY joining-fee headline under E7's new publiclyListed filter (publiclyListed is a query filter only on the gated fee embeds; guarded on = false so it is a no-op for already-listed types; touches at most two built-in config rows). No index, no foreign-key, no destructive SQL. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no annualFees field and never reads or writes it; the backfills only widen the values the NEW colour reads to match E6-era public output. Safe at any traffic level.

Additive email magic-link sign-in migration (#2034, epic #2030): ALTER TABLE ClubModuleSettings ADD COLUMN magicLink (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false) and CREATE TABLE MagicLinkToken (tokenHash unique, memberId, expiresAt, used, createdAt) with a unique index on tokenHash, a btree index on memberId, and an ON DELETE CASCADE FK to Member(id). The ADD COLUMN carries a constant default, so it is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) on the cold single-row (id=default) ClubModuleSettings settings table, which is not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX. The new MagicLinkToken table and its indexes build empty. The migration is flagged by the hot-table regex only because the new FK REFERENCES Member; ADD CONSTRAINT on a brand-new, empty child table validates nothing against Member and takes only a brief lock to register the constraint (no scan of Member). No backfill, no provider call, no destructive SQL. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no magicLink field and no MagicLinkToken model and never reads or writes either; the flag defaults OFF so magic-link sign-in stays disabled through the migrate->cutover window until an admin enables the module. Safe at any traffic level.

Additive Google OAuth sign-in migration (#2035, epic #2030): ALTER TABLE ClubModuleSettings ADD COLUMN googleLogin (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false) and ALTER TABLE Member ADD COLUMN googleSub (nullable TEXT) with a UNIQUE index Member_googleSub_key. The googleLogin ADD COLUMN carries a constant default, so it is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock) on the cold single-row (id=default) ClubModuleSettings table, which is not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX. The googleSub ADD COLUMN takes NO default, so it is likewise a metadata-only catalog change (no row scan, no rewrite) even though ALTER TABLE names the hot Member table — flagged by the hot-table regex only for naming Member. The unique index builds over an all-NULL column (NULLs never collide), so it is a fast, trivially-distinct build that briefly blocks Member writes; switch to CREATE UNIQUE INDEX CONCURRENTLY if Member is very large. No backfill, no provider call, no destructive SQL. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no googleLogin field and no Member.googleSub field and never reads or writes either; the flag defaults OFF so Google sign-in stays disabled through the migrate->cutover window until an admin enables the module AND a member links their account from their profile. Safe at any traffic level.

Additive per-age-tier subscription-behavior migration (#2041): ALTER TYPE "MembershipTypeSubscriptionBehavior" ADD VALUE IF NOT EXISTS 'BASED_ON_AGE_TIER'. Pure enum-label registration — no table, column, index, FK, backfill, provider call, or destructive SQL, and no existing MembershipType row is changed (every seeded/existing type keeps REQUIRED or NOT_REQUIRED). ALTER TYPE ADD VALUE cannot run in a transaction that also USES the new label, but this migration only registers it (never references it), so Prisma's per-migration transaction is safe; IF NOT EXISTS makes re-runs idempotent. Not a hot table (MembershipType is a cold admin-only config table, absent from HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX). Old-colour compatible: additive enum values are safe because no old colour is asked to read a BASED_ON_AGE_TIER row during the migrate->cutover window — the label is only ever written by the new colour's membership-types editor after cutover, so old Prisma clients keep deserialising the unchanged REQUIRED/NOT_REQUIRED rows (same additive-enum pattern as 20260525000000 and 20260702170000). Safe at any traffic level.

Additive per-age-tier annual-fee migration (#2067): ALTER TABLE MembershipAnnualFee ADD COLUMN ageTier ("AgeTier", NULLABLE, no default) plus index/constraint reshaping to mirror the JoiningFee flat-vs-tier keying — DROP the (membershipTypeId, effectiveFrom) unique and CREATE the (membershipTypeId, ageTier, effectiveFrom) unique, one raw-SQL partial unique (WHERE "ageTier" IS NULL), a widened effective-lookup btree including ageTier, TWO partial GiST no-overlap EXCLUDE constraints replacing the single per-type EXCLUDE — MembershipAnnualFee_flat_no_overlap (WHERE ageTier IS NULL) and MembershipAnnualFee_tier_no_overlap (adds "ageTier" WITH =, WHERE ageTier IS NOT NULL) — so flat-vs-flat and same-tier overlaps still conflict while flat-vs-tier and cross-tier coexist; the single COALESCE("ageTier"::text,'') EXCLUDE first drafted is ILLEGAL because an enum->text cast is only STABLE and Postgres rejects non-IMMUTABLE functions in an index/EXCLUDE expression (SQLSTATE 42P17); btree_gist supports enum equality natively on PG16 so the tier constraint needs no cast. The partial unique index AND both partial EXCLUDE-backed GiST indexes are recorded in prisma/partial-unique-indexes.tsv (the check-partial-indexes.sh set-equality gate inventories every partial index). Plus a raw-SQL CHECK forbidding PER_FAMILY per-tier rows. NO DML/backfill: a nullable ADD COLUMN with no default is a PostgreSQL 11+ catalog-only change (no table rewrite, no row scan, brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock); every existing row keeps ageTier NULL and IS the flat row, so resolution stays byte-identical. The index DROP/CREATE and EXCLUDE swap take brief ACCESS EXCLUSIVE / SHARE locks on the cold MembershipAnnualFee config table (not in HOT_TABLE_SQL_REGEX); run during the normal deploy window and let the deploy guard stop on lock timeout. No session clock (now()/CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) is used — the migration writes no rows. Old-colour compatible: the previously deployed Prisma client has no ageTier field and resolves fees WITHOUT filtering on ageTier — pre-cutover every row is a flat (NULL-tier) row, so it reads/writes exactly the flat rows, the pre-feature behaviour; the reshaped indexes/constraints only widen the keyspace the new colour uses. CAVEAT: do NOT create per-tier rows until the cutover completes — because the OLD resolver does NOT filter by ageTier, a per-tier row is NOT invisible to it: once such a row falls in an active window the OLD colour can SELECT it for ANY member regardless of tier (first match by effectiveFrom desc) and MIS-PRICE them at the wrong tier's amount (over-resolve), not merely skip it. No provider call, no destructive DML.
